headline: Turkey post-baseline confirm. Published 22.5 / Developing band reflects April 30 2026 first-agent baseline (downgrade from 32.8 applied) anchored on cumulative 432 ECtHR Article 10 violations + April 14-29 in-window pattern (5 sentenced journalists, 1 arrested, EFJ Article 217/A condemnation). May 2026 RSF Press Freedom Index update: Turkey 163/180 (down from 159 in 2025) — confirms baseline trajectory; 13 journalists detained in Q1 2026 alone; 26 currently imprisoned; Amnesty March 2026 Article 299 documentation. Sub-threshold drift; baseline holds. Recommend confirm at 22.5.
key_evidence: RSF 2026 World Press Freedom Index: Turkey 163/180 — down from 159 in 2025; sustained deterioration trajectory.
13 journalists detained in Q1 2026 alone; 26 journalists currently imprisoned (IFJ/EFJ).
Amnesty International March 2026 documentation: systematic Article 299 (insulting president) crackdown despite 2021 ECtHR ruling.
Cumulative 432 ECtHR Article 10 violations since 2002 (April 29 2026 publication baseline).
January 2026 IFJ/EFJ joint statement on escalating repression.

evidence.finding: Systematic Article 299 prosecution pattern despite 2021 ECtHR incompatibility ruling.
Government control over courts; persistent ECtHR non-compliance.

scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.6 confirmed. Article 299 prosecution pattern + ECtHR non-compliance sustained from April 30 baseline.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.4 confirmed. Declared CoE / ECHR commitments contradicted by 432 cumulative Article 10 violations + Article 299 persistence.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction matches published 22.5 exactly. No math-hygiene flag.

negativeSignals: RSF 163/180 deterioration; 13 Q1 2026 detentions; 26 currently imprisoned; Article 299 persistence; Article 217/A disinformation-law expansion; 432 cumulative ECtHR violations.
positiveSignals: Continued refugee hosting (~3M Syrians); basic universal services functional; some ECtHR compensation paid in individual cases; opposition municipal governance (Istanbul, Ankara, İzmir) operational despite political pressure.
sector_context: Press-freedom 2026 deterioration cluster with India (157/180). Turkey distinguished by structural ECtHR non-compliance pattern (432 cumulative violations).
open_actions_carried: Watch: 2026 election conduct and journalist treatment
